duplicatedParagraphs.text: By subscribing, you join the list for future China trip updates. Your checklist opens on the next page. Read our Privacy Policy .
A compact PDF covering pre-flight documents, arrival day, payment, apps, hotel addresses, transport, food, and emergency phrases.
A practical introduction to mobile payments, cash, cards, and what to set up before your first meal in China.
The essential apps for maps, translation, mobile payment, ride-hailing, trains, restaurants, and staying connected.
A practical foreign visitor's guide to booking Chinese high-speed trains, choosing the right station, using your passport, boarding, luggage, seat classes, and missed-train backups.
The China Payment & Apps Setup Guide gives you printable checklists, payment failure backup steps, app stack notes, and taxi or checkout phrase cards before your first day in China.
A strong first China route: Beijing for imperial history and the Great Wall, Xi'an for ancient heritage, Shanghai for modern China, and Hangzhou for lake scenery.
Save your hotel address, main attractions, railway station, airport or arrival point, and one backup restaurant area in Chinese so taxi drivers, hotel staff, and map apps can help faster.

A visa-free-friendly route centered on Shanghai and nearby Yangtze River Delta cities. Travelers must confirm current eligibility, entry city rules, onward ticket requirements, and permitted travel area before booking.
Use the printable checklist for documents, payment, apps, hotel address, transport, packing, and emergency phrases.
A printable setup pack for Alipay, WeChat Pay, essential apps, payment backups, and first-day troubleshooting.
First-time visitors who feel anxious about mobile payments, Chinese apps, taxis, and what to do if something fails on arrival day.

The free checklist tells you what to prepare. This guide gives you printable setup checklists, decision trees, phrase cards, an app stack, and a first-day troubleshooting table for payment and transport friction.
You can review every preview page now. Please contact us if you want to be notified when the Payhip checkout link is restored.
A cautious planning guide to China's 240-hour visa-free transit policy, including eligibility checks, onward tickets, permitted areas, ports, and common mistakes.
A comfortable Yangtze River Delta itinerary using short high-speed rail hops from Shanghai to Hangzhou and Suzhou.
A tourist-focused Alipay setup guide covering cards, QR codes, ride-hailing, restaurants, small purchases, limits, and backup payment habits.
A pre-flight checklist for first-time China visitors covering documents, visa checks, payment, apps, eSIM, hotel addresses, transport, food, packing, and emergency phrases.
These recommendations match the setup described in this guide. Compare the provider details with your own device, route, dates, and coverage needs.
A compact history-focused route connecting Beijing's imperial landmarks and Great Wall with Xi'an's Terracotta Warriors and old city atmosphere.
Compare options only after your dates and arrival station are clear. Save every confirmed address and booking reference offline before the trip.
A practical packing checklist for documents, mobile data, payment backups, train travel, weather, and daily comfort.
Simple Mandarin phrases for taxis, restaurants, hotels, train stations, payment problems, and polite daily interactions.
How to prepare mobile data, eSIM, roaming, Wi-Fi, offline screenshots, and app access before your first arrival day in China.
A practical guide to using WeChat Pay as a foreign visitor, including setup expectations, QR payments, mini programs, restaurants, taxis, and backups.

Your answers stay in this browser; this tool does not ask for a passport number, bank details, or an account. Results are planning guidance only. Always verify official requirements, booking rules, and current local conditions before paying for travel.
A focused Beijing itinerary balancing major historic landmarks, a Great Wall day, hutong walks, and classic local meals.
A practical Beijing first-trip kit with a softer pace: one arrival day, one Forbidden City core day, one Great Wall day, and one flexible museum or neighborhood day.
2-3 days is a realistic first-visit range. Add more time if you want day trips, slower food plans, or buffer for jet lag and weather.
A confident first China itinerary with classic Shanghai highlights, local food, modern neighborhoods, and a gentle day-trip option.
A practical planning guide for U.S. passport holders considering China travel in 2026, including visa planning, passport checks, flights, hotels, and official verification.
A practical first-time Shanghai plan with arrival setup, The Bund, Yu Garden, French Concession, food, metro tips, rainy-day backups, and what to skip.
1-2 days is a realistic first-visit range. Add more time if you want day trips, slower food plans, or buffer for jet lag and weather.
A practical guide to menus, QR ordering, spice levels, dietary phrases, payment, street food, and easy first meals in China.
